other_observers: David Leatherman found it in the yard and pointed it out to me; David Leatherman was the original finder and identifier
specific_location: in my farm yard 5 miles south of Lamar on Highway 287 and a little over a quarter of a mile to the west
description: -olive crown, head, back and cheeks -yellow spectacles -lighter neck patch -dark grayish wings with two white wing bars -dark grayish tail with white outer feathers -bright yellow throat and breast with blurry streaking on sides -white lower belly and undertail coverts -dark legs and black billBehaviors: -for the most part it stayed high in the trees -twice we thought it left and went west but it returned -I would have difficulty pointing to any specific behavior other than "warbler like"Call: Dave Leatherman heard it - I would have difficulty identifying it by songPlumage: adult male
